content.challenge: Identify and solve a significant gap in the strength equipment market: the lack of a modular weight-loading pin. Existing solutions were monolithic/fixed-length, forcing users to carry multiple pins for varying machine clearances and hole diameters (8mm vs 10mm).
content.approach: Engineered a "Base + Extender" system that allowed for a variable moment arm. This was the first solution to offer a telescoping 4.5" to 7" form factor in a single package. Developed a quick-swap mechanical interface for 8mm and 10mm rods. Utilized Design for Additive Manufacturing (DFAM) to optimize print orientations, ensuring primary shear forces were perpendicular to layer lines.
content.impact: Established a new product category in the "adder weight" niche, achieving six-figure revenue within 10 months. Dynamic stress testing under peak impulse revealed the elastic deformation limits of the polymer interface, providing the baseline requirements for the V2 overhaul.

content.challenge: Perform a ground-up mechanical overhaul to transition from a consumer-grade polymer tool to a "Zero-Failure" commercial-grade system. The goal was to increase the Factor of Safety (FoS) to 2.5x and eliminate all potential failure modes identified in the V1 lifecycle.
content.approach: Transitioned to CNC-machined 6061-T6 Anodized Aluminum for the bodies and 4140 Case Hardened Alloy Steel for the pins. Conducted FEA to analyze Von Mises stress at the rod-to-body interface, filleting all sharp transitions to reduce stress risers. Optimized the magnetic circuit using N52 Neodymium magnets. Utilized an interference fit (H7/p6) for the rod-to-body press-fit.
content.impact: Verified a 33% increase in rated capacity (to 180 lbs) with a validated 2.5x Factor of Safety for dynamic peak loads. Reduced field failure rates to 0% by shifting to CNC-machined components and implementing a PPAP-lite inspection protocol for all mission-critical dimensions.
